title: After-Action Report — Post-game card decisions + team records, and the "(L, 4-0)" box-score bug
scope: Three tangled complaints about how finished games present pitcher decisions and records:

rationale.item: Box scores showed impossible decision records — e.g. `Hlavacka (L, 4-0)`: a pitcher tagged with the loss whose season record didn't include a loss. And "sometimes the record is there, sometimes it isn't."
The expanded (schedule) cards still showed the SP matchup post-game, while the small (dashboard) cards already switched to W/L pitchers with records "like real MLB."
No team records on the cards — the user wanted the W-L line on each side, pre-game and updated after the game resolves.

Root cause of #1: two different W/L attribution algorithms — the box score labeled the losing pitcher by most runs allowed, but the record came from the season W-L map which charges the loss by most earned runs.

delegation_events.decision: Finisher line requested: save slot shows finisher with terminal-outs total
delegation_events.quote: The owner asked for the save slot on the scorecard to show the finisher with his terminal-outs total, like a record: `F: Brockman (33)`

delegation_events.decision: Out-share-based win attribution flagged back to owner rather than implemented
delegation_events.quote: Changing that needs per-pitcher "outs-while-leading" data the schema doesn't expose yet, so it's flagged, not silently rewritten.

actions.description: One source of truth for decisions: new pure helpers credit_win / charge_loss / decide_pitchers; SP_OUTS_THRESHOLD moved here

All surfaces route through the canonical helpers: _pitcher_wl_map, _attach_decisions (aggregating pitcher rows per player across phases), new _game_decision_pids, game_detail, youth/world-cup box views, box_text._pick_decisions
render_pitching_table reserves room for the decision/record suffix and truncates the name instead

Cards: new _attach_team_records(games, as_of_date) attaching away_record/home_record; wired into index and schedule routes; record chips and W/L decision blocks in templates; SP matchup shown only pre-game

Finisher line: new box_score.pick_finisher(win_rows); multi-tag composition in render_pitching_table and box_text._pitching_block; new app._game_finisher and _terminal_outs_map; f_pitcher attached in _attach_decisions; cards render F: line

risk_posture: The W/L *heuristics themselves* (12-out SP win, most-ER loss) are unchanged in spirit — the win here was making every surface agree on one of them, not inventing a new rule.
Saves-by-the-old-rule remain absent on purpose; the finisher line is the intended replacement.

validation_claims.claim: Reproduced the bug on a seeded 30-team DB (120 finals): 30 label/record mismatches, 26 labels with no matching season record. After the fix: 0 losers without a loss, 0 winners without a win; season totals stay balanced (total_W == total_L == finals)
validation_claims.check_method: Seed a 30-team DB, sim 120 finals, cross-check decision labels vs season W-L map

validation_claims.claim: Long-name case now renders `Gymnastikfor (L, 0-1)` (name truncated, record intact)
validation_claims.check_method: Render a pitching table with a long pitcher name

validation_claims.claim: Rendered /, /schedule, and /game/<id> against the seeded DB: records on both card styles pre/post game, W/L decisions on expanded finals, SP matchup retained for upcoming games
validation_claims.check_method: Run the app against a seeded DB and inspect the three routes

validation_claims.claim: On a seeded 120-final season: 77 games have a finisher, 21 of them a pitcher who also won
validation_claims.check_method: Query game_pitcher_stats terminal-outs leaders vs win attribution on a seeded season

non_actions.reason: Changing that needs per-pitcher "outs-while-leading" data the schema doesn't expose yet, so it's flagged, not silently rewritten.
the win here was making every surface agree on one of them, not inventing a new rule

non_actions.item: Per-game historical replay of team records for arbitrary mixed-date lists
non_actions.reason: Team records on a card are "as of the card's anchor date." On the dashboard/schedule every game in a list shares that date, so the figure is exact

residual_risks: Open question raised back to the owner: the engine's current win attribution is SP if ≥12 outs, else most-effective reliever — not the out-share-of-lead computation the owner described.
open_hooks: Possible future out-share-based win rule (needs per-pitcher outs-while-leading schema data)
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Post-game card decisions + team records, and the "(L, 4-0)" box-score bug
Follow-up: the finisher ("F") line — O27's save
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/postgame-scorecard-display-dMhTC. Detail on the split: app.game_detail built decisions inline (W = last winning pitcher who recorded an out; L = losing pitcher with most runs_allowed) while app._pitcher_wl_map charged W = SP if ≥12 outs else most-effective reliever; L = pitcher with most er. 30 of 120 simulated finals disagreed; 26 of those 30 showed no record at all. Second smaller cause: long-named decision pitcher overran the fixed-width name column and the record got truncated. Finisher definition: Terminal Outs (TO) — outs in a spell where the pitcher entered with a lead, never let it be tied or lost, and finished the game; a finisher can also be the winning pitcher, e.g. `Manue (W, 1-0) (F, 7)`. _attach_team_records: finals show the record through their date; unplayed games show what clubs bring in (through the prior day); one query covers both cutoffs.
parser_notes: Date from 'Date completed:' line. Title contains punctuation/quotes; slug shortened. Document includes a follow-up section (finisher line) appended after main validation.
